disease: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma
treatment: All reagents were dissolved in either DMSO or sterile water based on solubility requirements and included Copper(II) diethyldithiocarbamate at 85 mg/50 ml (VWR, Radnor, PA), 2-C1-IB-MECA at 20 mg/50 ml (Millipore, Burlington, MA), 2'3'-c-di-AM(PS)2 )Rp,Rp) at 715 ng/50 ul , 5’ppp-dsRNA/Lyovec at 375 ng/50 ml (Invivogen, San Diego, CA), R848 at 15 mg/50 ml (Invivogen), galunisertib at 30 mg/50 ml (Selleckchem, Houston, TX), epcadostat at 5 mg/50 ml (Selleckchem), Nw-Hydroxy-nor-L-arginine at 6 mg/50 ml (Sigma-Aldrich, St. Louis, MO), L-NMMA at 100 ng/50 ml (Abcam, Boston, MA), clodronate at 80 mg/50 ml (Abcam), and 4-1BB ligand at 1 mg/50 ml (NovusBio). Combinatorial treatments were reconstituted as a 10x stock, combined as necessary, and diluted to a 1x concentration with the appropriate diluent. Treatments were intratumorally injected with a 28 gauge needle as a 50 ml bolus.
